game.name: cue_n_woo
game.description: Two-player theory-of-mind game backed by a local FLAS/Gemma worker.

tokens.description: One connection token per player slot. Runner-injected.

require_signing.description: If true, the game fails to start unless it can load the worker signing key (so tournament priority is guaranteed). If false, a missing or unreadable key degrades to unsigned normal-priority requests. Tournaments should set true.

stub_worker.description: If true, the game returns deterministic canned worker responses instead of calling the LLM worker. Used by certification so the smoke test runs offline with no GPU/worker dependency. Never set in real play.

max_question_tokens.description: Simple whitespace-token limit for submitted questions/prompts. Questions may use normal prompt whitespace.

max_answer_tokens.description: Simple whitespace-token limit for player answers. Answers may contain printable keyboard characters and single spaces only.

max_prompt_tokens.description: Maximum tokenized prompt length sent to the LLM worker.

concept_type.description: list selects from data/concepts.json; specific uses specific_concept; random uses a seeded random FLAS vector.

concept_index.description: Optional fixed index into data/concepts.json when concept_type is list.

specific_concept.description: Exact text concept when concept_type is specific. This is server-private and is not sent to players.

concept_seed.description: Seed for random concept vectors. This is server-private and is not sent to players.

concept_list_path.description: Optional path to a JSON array of text concepts.

reveal_concept_to_clients.description: Whether final player/global state includes the hidden concept. Defaults false.

include_concept_in_results.description: Whether final results/replay artifacts include the hidden concept. Defaults false.

readme.value:
# Cue-n-Woo

A two-player theory-of-mind game. A hidden-style judge (a steered FLAS/Gemma model) answers questions in a secret persona. Each player privately interviews the judge, then writes challenge questions plus their own answers. Players also answer the opponent's challenge questions. The server scores how well each answer matches the judge's style, and the player who better predicts the judge wins.

## Flow

1. `private_questions`: each player asks the judge up to three private questions.
2. `proposals`: each player submits three challenge questions, each with their own answer.
3. `answers`: each player answers the opponent's three challenge questions. An empty answer is a permitted decline and scores 0; non-empty answers need at least three non-space characters.
4. `reveal`: the hidden concept is revealed and the round is scored.

The hidden concept is server-private during play.

content.value:
# Cue-n-Woo Rules

Two players independently ask a hidden-style judge up to three private questions. Each player then writes three challenge questions plus their own answers. Players also answer the opponent's challenge questions. The server scores answer likelihoods using the local FLAS/Gemma worker. The hidden concept is server-private during play.

# Playing Cue-n-Woo

Connect your player to the Coworld-provided `COWORLD_PLAYER_WS_URL`. Send `ask`, `propose`, and `answer` messages as described in the player protocol.

player.name: Baseline Player
player.description: Bedrock Claude LLM player harness; the model makes every decision via submit_action and retries validation errors.

player.name: Stub Player
player.description: Deterministic offline stub for certification; asks/proposes precommitted text and always answers from a backup list, no LLM or AWS.

player.name: Kyle Policy
player.description: Baseline LLM harness seeded with Kyle's optional per-phase question suggestions; the model may freely ignore them.

variants.name: Default
variants.description: Two-player default cue-n-woo game with a five-minute hard timer.
